title: The Skewer
summary: Like a pin — but in reverse

steps.title: The Skewer
steps.text: A skewer is the mirror image of a pin. You attack a valuable piece that MUST move — and when it does, a less valuable piece behind it is exposed. Most often the king is the skewered piece: he must move out of check, abandoning whatever was behind him.

steps.prompt: Bring your rook to the d-file to skewer king and queen.
steps.hint: Rd1 gives check — the queen will be next.

steps.title: Collect the Prize
steps.text: After Rd1+, the king must move. The queen is lost on the next move. This is why skewers are so powerful on open files and diagonals — even a well-placed rook can topple a queen.
